metadata.title: Watch int8 Rounding Mash Your Activations

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Watch int8 Rounding Mash Your Activations

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Kore, in for Humanitarians AI. Quantizing weights from negative 1.5 to 1.5 is harmless. Quantizing an activation from 0.001 to 0.01 destroys the signal — same 8 bits, opposite outcome.

props.command: claude "write quantize_demo.py: linear int8 quant (scale, zero_point). Case A weights in [-1.5,1.5], case B activations in [0.001,0.01]. Show what the rounding does to each."
props.segment: Watch int8 Rounding Mash Your Activations
output: Case B mash zone: many values → same bin

beats.narration_text: Here is why Watch int8 Rounding Mash Your Activations belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

props.command: claude "write quantize_demo.py: linear int8 quant (scale, zero_point). Case A weights in [-1.5,1.5], case B activations in [0.001,0.01]. Show what the rounding does to each."
props.segment: Watch int8 Rounding Mash Your Activations
output: Case B mash zone: many values → same bin

beats.narration_text: From the textbook: the quantization number-line diagram. That bottom strip — where a huge float range crushes into a handful of integer bins — is exactly what our CLI script detects.

beats.act: MANIM — Wide-range case A: smooth staircase
beats.narration_text: Case A: weights spanning negative 1.5 to 1.5. The int8 scale is 0.0118 per step. A sine wave across that range snaps to the grid with a staircase error of about 0.005. Harmless — the quantization barely disturbs the signal.

beats.act: MANIM — Narrow-range case B: mash zone
beats.narration_text: Case B: activations spanning 0.001 to 0.01. The int8 scale is 0.000039 — tiny. But the full meaningful range of the activation maps to just a few bins. Many distinct values collapse to the same integer bin. The mash zone. The signal is gone.

Side by side. Case A staircase hugs the original curve. Case B collapses — the narrow activation range occupies so few bins that the quantized output is nearly flat. This is why per-channel quantization exists: each channel gets its own scale, tuned to its own range.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write quantize_demo.py — linear int8 quantization with scale and zero point. Case A: weights in the range negative 1.5 to 1.5. Case B: activations in the range 0.001 to 0.01. Show what the rounding does to each. Run it and compare the two cases side by side. The mash zone in case B is why per-channel quantization exists. Now add a third case C that uses per-channel scales to rescue case B.

props.command: claude "write quantize_demo.py: linear int8 quant (scale, zero_point). Case A weights in [-1.5,1.5], case B activations in [0.001,0.01]. Show what the rounding does to each."

beats.narration_text: Kore, in for Humanitarians AI. From Humanitarians AI.
